intent: Check whether the candidate understands the special Express error-handling middleware signature and how it differs from normal middleware.
primaryQuestion: How is the signature of Express error-handling middleware different from normal middleware?
expectedSignals: States that normal middleware usually has the signature (req, res, next), while error-handling middleware has (err, req, res, next).
Explains that the first parameter is the error object or value passed by Express.
Mentions that Express uses this four-argument function shape to route errors to error handlers.
Understands that an error handler can inspect err, log it, map it to an HTTP response, or pass it onward with next(err).
Notes that the handler should still either send a response or delegate, otherwise the request can hang.
redFlags: Says an error handler has the same signature as normal middleware.
Places err after req or omits next entirely without understanding Express detection.
Thinks error handlers run for every request even when no error is present.
Cannot explain what the err parameter represents.

scoringHints.description: Clearly contrasts (req, res, next) with (err, req, res, next), explains Express routing to error handlers, and describes response or delegation responsibility.
Knows the four-argument signature and err-first shape but gives a shallow explanation of how Express selects the handler.
Has seen error middleware but cannot reliably describe the signature or execution conditions.
Answer does not show practical Express middleware knowledge.

followUps.question: Can you write the exact function signature for a global Express error handler?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete recall of the err-first four-argument signature.

followUps.question: If no error has been passed, will this error handler run for a normal successful request?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate understands error-path execution.

en.title: Express error middleware signature
en.displayQuestion: How is the signature of Express error-handling middleware different from normal middleware?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate knows the err-first four-argument middleware form.
guidance: Compare normal middleware with error-handling middleware directly.
Mention the exact order: err, req, res, next.
Explain that Express routes error flows to this handler shape.
Say what the handler should do after receiving the error.
commonMistakes: Using (req, res, next) for errors.
Putting err in the wrong position.
Assuming error handlers run on successful requests.
Logging the error but never ending or delegating the request.

vi.title: Chữ ký error middleware trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Error handling middleware trong Express có chữ ký gì khác với middleware thông thường?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có nắm dạng middleware 4 tham số với err đứng đầu hay không.
guidance: So sánh trực tiếp middleware thường với error-handling middleware.
Nêu đúng thứ tự tham số: err, req, res, next.
Giải thích Express dùng dạng này để đưa luồng lỗi vào error handler.
Nói rõ handler cần gửi response hoặc chuyển tiếp lỗi sau khi nhận err.
commonMistakes: Dùng (req, res, next) cho error handler.
Đặt err sai vị trí.
Tưởng error handler chạy cho cả request thành công.
Chỉ log lỗi nhưng không kết thúc hoặc không delegate request.

ja.title: Express error middleware signature
ja.displayQuestion: Express の error-handling middleware は通常の middleware と signature がどう違いますか。
ja.displayIntent: err が先頭に来る 4 引数 middleware form を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: 通常 middleware と error-handling middleware を直接比較する。
正しい順序 err, req, res, next を述べる。
Express がこの形の handler に error flow を渡すことを説明する。
error を受け取った handler が response を返すか委譲する必要があると説明する。
commonMistakes: error handler に (req, res, next) を使う。
err の位置を間違える。
成功 request でも error handler が実行されると思う。
error を log するだけで request を終了も委譲もしない。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for error-handling middleware signature and next(err) behavior.

intent: Verify whether the candidate knows why Express error-handling middleware must preserve four declared parameters even when some parameters are unused.
primaryQuestion: Why must Express error-handling middleware have exactly four parameters: err, req, res, next?
expectedSignals: Explains that Express distinguishes error-handling middleware from normal middleware by the declared four-argument function shape.
States that err must be the first argument and represents the error passed through the request pipeline.
Mentions that omitting next or another unused argument can make Express treat the function as normal middleware instead of an error handler.
Understands that unused arguments should still be kept, often with an underscore naming convention if lint rules allow it.
Mentions that minification, wrappers, or default/rest parameters can be risky if they change the function arity Express observes.
redFlags: Says four parameters are only a style preference.
Removes next because it is not currently used and cannot explain the consequence.
Thinks TypeScript types alone decide whether Express treats a function as an error handler.
Uses an async wrapper that hides the error-handler arity without testing it.

scoringHints.description: Explains Express arity-based detection, keeps all four parameters, and understands practical pitfalls around unused arguments and wrappers.
Knows that four arguments are required but misses arity or wrapper nuance.
Can repeat the signature but cannot explain why Express cares.
Answer is incorrect about how Express identifies error handlers.

followUps.question: What can happen if you define the handler as (err, req, res) and omit next?
How would you handle lint warnings for an unused next parameter while preserving Express behavior?

en.title: Why four error-handler parameters matter
en.displayQuestion: Why must Express error-handling middleware have exactly four parameters: err, req, res, next?
en.displayIntent: Check knowledge of Express error-handler detection and function arity.
guidance: Explain that Express detects error handlers by the four-argument form.
Keep err first and next last even when next is rarely used.
Mention that TypeScript types do not replace runtime function shape.
Discuss unused-parameter linting pragmatically.
commonMistakes: Removing unused next.
Treating the signature as cosmetic.
Depending only on TypeScript types.
Wrapping the handler in a way that changes its arity.

vi.title: Vì sao error handler cần đủ 4 tham số
vi.displayQuestion: Tại sao error handling middleware phải có đúng 4 tham số (err, req, res, next)?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết về cách Express nhận diện error handler qua số tham số khai báo.
guidance: Giải thích Express nhận diện error handler bằng dạng function 4 tham số.
Giữ err ở đầu và next ở cuối dù next ít khi dùng.
Nói rõ type TypeScript không thay thế được runtime function shape.
Nêu cách xử lý lint warning cho tham số không dùng một cách thực tế.
commonMistakes: Xóa next vì chưa dùng.
Xem chữ ký chỉ là style.
Dựa hoàn toàn vào TypeScript type.
Wrap handler khiến arity bị thay đổi.

ja.title: error handler に 4 引数が必要な理由
ja.displayQuestion: Express の error-handling middleware はなぜ (err, req, res, next) の 4 引数である必要がありますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express が function arity で error handler を識別することを理解しているか確認する。
guidance: Express が 4 引数 form で error handler を検出することを説明する。
next をほとんど使わなくても err を先頭、next を末尾に保つ。
TypeScript type だけでは runtime の function shape を置き換えられないと説明する。
unused parameter の lint 対応を実務的に述べる。
commonMistakes: unused の next を削除する。
signature を見た目の style として扱う。
TypeScript type だけに頼る。
handler を wrap して arity を変えてしまう。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for the four-argument error-handling middleware contract.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands how Express enters the error-handling path and the difference between next() and next(err).
primaryQuestion: How do you trigger Express error-handling middleware? Should you use next() or next(err)?
expectedSignals: States that next(err) triggers the error-handling path and skips normal non-error middleware and route handlers.
States that next() without an error continues the normal middleware chain.
Mentions that throwing a synchronous error inside a route or middleware also triggers Express error handling.
Mentions that in Express 5 a rejected Promise from a handler is forwarded automatically, while Express 4 needs explicit forwarding.
Understands that passing any value except the special string 'route' to next is treated as an error.
redFlags: Uses next() after catching an error and expects the error handler to run.
Sends an error response in every route instead of delegating consistent errors.
Calls next(err) after already sending a normal response without guarding control flow.
Cannot distinguish skipping normal handlers from continuing the normal chain.

scoringHints.description: Accurately distinguishes next() and next(err), includes throw and rejected Promise behavior, and understands pipeline skipping.
Knows next(err) is needed but misses one trigger path such as synchronous throw or Express 5 promises.
Recognizes next but cannot reason about normal versus error middleware chains.
Answer would lead to lost errors or incorrect middleware execution.

followUps.question: What happens to normal middleware registered after the current handler when you call next(err)?
If you catch an error and call next() with no argument, what response path will Express take?
followUps.purpose: Expose misunderstanding between normal and error flow.

en.title: Triggering Express error handlers
en.displayQuestion: How do you trigger Express error-handling middleware? Should you use next() or next(err)?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate knows how Express switches from the normal pipeline to the error pipeline.
guidance: State that next(err) enters the error path.
Contrast it with next() continuing normal execution.
Mention synchronous throw and Express 5 Promise rejection behavior.
Explain what happens to remaining normal handlers.
commonMistakes: Calling next() after catching an error.
Forgetting that next(err) skips normal handlers.
Calling next(err) after sending a success response.
Treating all async errors the same across Express 4 and 5.

vi.title: Kích hoạt error handler trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để trigger error handling middleware? Dùng next() hay next(err)?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có biết cách Express chuyển từ pipeline thường sang pipeline lỗi hay không.
guidance: Nói rõ next(err) đưa request vào error path.
So sánh với next() là tiếp tục luồng bình thường.
Nhắc tới synchronous throw và behavior Promise rejection trong Express 5.
Giải thích các handler thường còn lại sẽ bị bỏ qua như thế nào.
commonMistakes: Catch lỗi rồi gọi next() không truyền lỗi.
Quên rằng next(err) bỏ qua normal handler.
Gọi next(err) sau khi đã gửi success response.
Xem async error giống nhau giữa Express 4 và Express 5.

ja.title: Express error handler を起動する
ja.displayQuestion: Express の error-handling middleware はどう trigger しますか。next() と next(err) のどちらを使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express が通常 pipeline から error pipeline に切り替わる条件を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: next(err) が error path に入ることを述べる。
next() は通常 flow を継続すると対比する。
synchronous throw と Express 5 の Promise rejection behavior に触れる。
残りの normal handler がどう扱われるか説明する。
commonMistakes: error を catch した後に next() だけを呼ぶ。
next(err) が normal handler を skip することを忘れる。
success response 送信後に next(err) を呼ぶ。
Express 4 と Express 5 の async error を同じものとして扱う。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for next(err), thrown errors, and promise rejection behavior.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands the different handling paths for synchronous errors, callback-based asynchronous errors, and promise-based errors in Express.
primaryQuestion: How are synchronous errors and asynchronous errors handled differently in Express?
expectedSignals: Explains that Express catches synchronous exceptions thrown during route or middleware execution.
Explains that asynchronous callback errors must be passed to next(err) because the original synchronous call stack has already returned.
Mentions that promise rejections are automatically forwarded in Express 5, but not in Express 4 without a wrapper or explicit catch.
Distinguishes errors thrown inside setTimeout, callbacks, event listeners, or background jobs from errors thrown directly in the handler body.
Uses concrete patterns such as fs callbacks calling next(err), try/catch around awaited work in Express 4, or Promise.resolve(fn).catch(next).
redFlags: Assumes Express catches every error anywhere in the process.
Throws inside a timer or callback and expects Express to catch it automatically.
Does not distinguish Express 4 from Express 5 promise behavior.
Uses process-level handlers as a substitute for request-level error handling.

scoringHints.description: Explains stack timing, callback errors, promises, Express 4 versus 5 behavior, and gives safe code patterns.
Understands sync versus async broadly but misses one category such as timers, event emitters, or Express version differences.
Knows to use next(err) but cannot explain why async errors need explicit forwarding.
Answer would likely drop async errors or crash the process unexpectedly.

followUps.question: Why does an error thrown inside setTimeout not behave like an error thrown directly inside app.get?
Show how you would pass an fs.readFile error to the Express error handler.

en.title: Synchronous and asynchronous Express errors
en.displayQuestion: How are synchronous errors and asynchronous errors handled differently in Express?
en.displayIntent: Assess request-level error propagation across sync code, callbacks, timers, and promises.
guidance: Start with Express automatically catching synchronous throws in handlers.
Explain why async callbacks must call next(err).
Separate Express 4 and Express 5 promise behavior.
Mention timers, event emitters, and background work as outside normal request flow.
commonMistakes: Expecting Express to catch all process errors.
Throwing inside a callback instead of calling next(err).
Ignoring Express version differences.
Using global process handlers for normal route errors.

vi.title: Synchronous và asynchronous error trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Synchronous error và asynchronous error được xử lý khác nhau như thế nào trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá cách ứng viên hiểu propagation lỗi trong request qua code sync, callback, timer và promise.
guidance: Bắt đầu từ việc Express tự bắt lỗi throw đồng bộ trong handler.
Giải thích vì sao async callback phải gọi next(err).
Tách rõ behavior promise giữa Express 4 và Express 5.
Nhắc timer, event emitter và background work là ngoài request flow thông thường.
commonMistakes: Kỳ vọng Express bắt mọi lỗi trong process.
Throw trong callback thay vì gọi next(err).
Bỏ qua khác biệt giữa các version Express.
Dùng global process handler cho lỗi route thông thường.

ja.title: Express の sync error と async error
ja.displayQuestion: Express では synchronous error と asynchronous error はどのように違って処理されますか。
ja.displayIntent: sync code, callbacks, timers, promises における request-level error propagation を評価する。
guidance: handler 内の synchronous throw は Express が自動的に捕捉することから説明する。
async callback では next(err) が必要な理由を説明する。
Express 4 と Express 5 の promise behavior を分けて説明する。
timer, event emitter, background work は通常の request flow 外だと述べる。
commonMistakes: Express が process 内のすべての error を catch すると考える。
callback 内で next(err) ではなく throw する。
Express version の違いを無視する。
通常の route error に global process handler を使う。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for synchronous errors, asynchronous callback patterns, and Express 5 promise forwarding.

sourceReferences.label: Node.js docs: Process events
sourceReferences.note: Used for context around uncaught exceptions and unhandled promise rejections.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can diagnose and fix unhandled promise rejections from async route handlers in Express 4.
primaryQuestion: If an async route handler throws an error, does Express 4 catch it automatically? How would you handle it?
expectedSignals: States that Express 4 does not automatically catch rejected promises or thrown errors from async route handlers.
Explains that the error becomes a rejected Promise unless it is caught and passed to next(err).
Shows a try/catch around await and calls next(err) in the catch block.
Mentions reusable wrappers such as asyncHandler(fn) returning (req, res, next) => Promise.resolve(fn(req, res, next)).catch(next).
Mentions package-based approaches such as express-async-errors or express-async-handler, while noting version and project policy considerations.
redFlags: Claims Express 4 automatically handles async/await errors like Express 5.
Adds process.on('unhandledRejection') and considers the route fixed.
Swallows the error in catch and returns a success response.
Forgets next in the async handler signature and cannot pass the error onward.

scoringHints.description: Correctly identifies Express 4 promise behavior and gives safe fixes using try/catch, a wrapper, or a deliberate package approach.
Knows to catch and call next(err) but misses reusable wrapper or package trade-offs.
Has hit the problem but applies inconsistent per-route fixes or global process handlers.
Answer would still produce unhandled promise rejections in Express 4.

followUps.question: Can you write a small asyncHandler wrapper for Express 4?
Why is process.on('unhandledRejection') not a good replacement for calling next(err) in a route?
followUps.purpose: Separate request-level error handling from process-level safety nets.

en.title: Async route errors in Express 4
en.displayQuestion: If an async route handler throws an error, does Express 4 catch it automatically? How would you handle it?
en.displayIntent: Check practical debugging of async/await errors in Express 4.
guidance: State clearly that Express 4 does not auto-forward async handler rejections.
Show try/catch with next(err).
Mention a reusable Promise wrapper.
Discuss packages only as a deliberate project-level choice.
commonMistakes: Assuming Express 4 behaves like Express 5.
Using only global unhandledRejection handlers.
Swallowing the error.
Forgetting next in the handler.

vi.title: Async route error trong Express 4
vi.displayQuestion: Nếu một async route handler throw error, Express 4 có tự bắt không? Làm thế nào để xử lý?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra khả năng debug lỗi async/await trong Express 4.
guidance: Nói rõ Express 4 không tự forward rejection từ async handler.
Đưa ví dụ try/catch rồi gọi next(err).
Nhắc tới wrapper Promise tái sử dụng.
Chỉ dùng package như một quyết định cấp project, không phải vá tạm không kiểm soát.
commonMistakes: Tưởng Express 4 giống Express 5.
Chỉ dùng global unhandledRejection handler.
Nuốt lỗi trong catch.
Quên khai báo next trong handler.

ja.title: Express 4 の async route error
ja.displayQuestion: async route handler が error を throw した場合、Express 4 は自動的に catch しますか。どう処理しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express 4 における async/await error の実務的な debugging 能力を確認する。
guidance: Express 4 は async handler rejection を自動 forward しないと明確に述べる。
try/catch で next(err) を呼ぶ例を示す。
再利用可能な Promise wrapper に触れる。
package は project-level の意図的な選択として説明する。
commonMistakes: Express 4 が Express 5 と同じ挙動だと考える。
global unhandledRejection handler だけを使う。
catch で error を握りつぶす。
handler に next を宣言し忘れる。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express error propagation and Express 5 promise behavior comparison.

sourceReferences.label: npm: express-async-errors
sourceReferences.note: Used for the package-level async error patching approach.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands what express-async-errors solves and the trade-offs of patch-based async error handling in Express 4 applications.
primaryQuestion: What problem does the express-async-errors package solve?
expectedSignals: Explains that express-async-errors lets errors thrown from async Express route handlers or middleware reach the normal Express error handler in Express 4-style apps.
Mentions that it works by patching Express router/layer behavior so returned rejected promises are forwarded.
States that it reduces repetitive try/catch or async wrapper boilerplate in route handlers.
Mentions that the app still needs a centralized error handler registered after routes.
Notes trade-offs: monkey patching, dependency maintenance, version compatibility, and that Express 5 has built-in promise rejection forwarding.
redFlags: Thinks the package creates error responses automatically without an error handler.
Uses it together with ad hoc per-route catches inconsistently and produces double next calls.
Does not know it is mainly an Express 4 async/await pain-point workaround.
Cannot discuss the risk of patching framework internals.

scoringHints.description: Explains the Express 4 async error gap, how the package patches forwarding, why it reduces boilerplate, and when Express 5 makes it unnecessary.
Knows it forwards async errors but misses patching mechanics or version trade-offs.
Has used the package but cannot explain what problem it solves.
Answer misrepresents the package as validation, logging, or response formatting.

followUps.question: What would you consider before adding a monkey-patching package to a production Express app?
After importing express-async-errors, do you still need app.use((err, req, res, next) => ...)? Why?
followUps.purpose: Check separation between error forwarding and error response formatting.

en.title: express-async-errors purpose
en.displayQuestion: What problem does the express-async-errors package solve?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands Express 4 async error forwarding and package trade-offs.
guidance: Connect the package to async/await route errors in Express 4.
Explain that it forwards rejected promises to Express error middleware.
Mention the remaining need for a centralized error handler.
Discuss monkey-patching and Express 5 compatibility considerations.
commonMistakes: Thinking it formats error responses.
Forgetting to register an error handler.
Ignoring framework patching risks.
Using it without understanding Express version behavior.

vi.title: Mục đích của express-async-errors
vi.displayQuestion: express-async-errors package dùng để giải quyết vấn đề gì?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên hiểu việc forward lỗi async trong Express 4 và trade-off khi dùng package.
guidance: Liên hệ package với lỗi async/await trong route Express 4.
Giải thích package forward rejected promise tới Express error middleware.
Nhắc vẫn cần centralized error handler.
Nói về monkey patching và cân nhắc khi dùng Express 5.
commonMistakes: Tưởng package format response lỗi.
Quên đăng ký error handler.
Bỏ qua rủi ro patch framework.
Dùng mà không hiểu behavior theo version Express.

ja.title: express-async-errors の目的
ja.displayQuestion: express-async-errors package はどの問題を解決しますか。
ja.displayIntent: Express 4 の async error forwarding と package の trade-off を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: package を Express 4 の async/await route error と結びつける。
rejected promise を Express error middleware に forward すると説明する。
centralized error handler は依然として必要だと述べる。
monkey patching と Express 5 での扱いを議論する。
commonMistakes: error response を format する package だと思う。
error handler 登録を忘れる。
framework patching の risk を無視する。
Express version behavior を理解せず使う。

sourceReferences.label: npm: express-async-errors
sourceReferences.note: Used for the package purpose and async/await error handling behavior.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express error middleware and built-in Express 5 promise forwarding comparison.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can design a centralized Express error handler that produces a consistent API error contract without leaking internals.
primaryQuestion: How would you create a centralized error handler that returns a consistent response format?
expectedSignals: Registers a single error-handling middleware after routes and 404 handling.
Normalizes errors into a consistent response envelope such as { error: { code, message, requestId } }.
Maps known application errors to status codes and safe public messages.
Logs internal details server-side while hiding stack traces and sensitive data from clients in production.
Handles res.headersSent by delegating to Express default error handling or the next error handler.
Includes operational fields such as request ID, error code, validation details when safe, and environment-specific stack output only when appropriate.
redFlags: Copies err.stack directly into production responses.
Creates different ad hoc error shapes in every route.
Does not handle headersSent or duplicate response paths.
Returns 500 for every known client-side validation or auth error.
Logs sensitive request bodies, credentials, tokens, or personally identifiable data without filtering.

scoringHints.description: Designs a consistent error envelope, status mapping, safe logging, headersSent handling, validation details, and environment-aware exposure.
Has a centralized handler and consistent response shape but misses one production detail such as headersSent, request IDs, or sensitive logging.
Understands centralization but response mapping and logging are still inconsistent or unsafe.
Answer would produce inconsistent API contracts or leak internal errors.

followUps.question: What JSON would your handler return for a validation error versus an unexpected database error?
followUps.purpose: Check concrete response format and status mapping.

followUps.question: What should your handler do if res.headersSent is already true?

en.title: Centralized Express error response
en.displayQuestion: How would you create a centralized error handler that returns a consistent response format?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate API error contract design, production safety, and Express lifecycle handling.
guidance: Place the error middleware after routes and 404 handling.
Normalize every error into one response envelope.
Map known errors to status codes and safe messages.
Log internals separately and handle headersSent correctly.
commonMistakes: Returning err.stack to clients.
Using a different error shape in every route.
Ignoring headersSent.
Treating validation errors and server bugs the same.

vi.title: Centralized error response trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để tạo một centralized error handler trả về response format thống nhất?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá thiết kế API error contract, an toàn production và xử lý lifecycle của Express.
guidance: Đặt error middleware sau routes và 404 handling.
Chuẩn hóa mọi lỗi về một response envelope.
Map lỗi đã biết sang status code và message an toàn.
Log chi tiết nội bộ riêng và xử lý headersSent đúng.
commonMistakes: Trả err.stack cho client.
Mỗi route dùng một error shape khác nhau.
Bỏ qua headersSent.
Xử lý validation error và bug server giống nhau.

ja.title: Express の centralized error response
ja.displayQuestion: 統一された response format を返す centralized error handler はどう作りますか。
ja.displayIntent: API error contract design, production safety, Express lifecycle handling を評価する。
guidance: error middleware は routes と 404 handling の後に置く。
すべての error を一つの response envelope に normalize する。
既知の error を status code と安全な message に map する。
内部詳細は別に log し、headersSent を正しく扱う。
commonMistakes: err.stack を client に返す。
route ごとに異なる error shape を使う。
headersSent を無視する。
validation error と server bug を同じように扱う。

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can classify errors by recoverability and choose appropriate Express and process-level handling strategies.
primaryQuestion: What is the difference between operational errors and programmer errors?
expectedSignals: Defines operational errors as expected runtime failures that a well-written program must handle, such as validation failures, unauthorized access, missing resources, timeouts, rate limits, or downstream service failures.
Defines programmer errors as bugs in code such as TypeError, invariant violations, wrong assumptions, undefined access, bad state mutation, or incorrect API usage.
Explains that operational errors can usually be converted into controlled HTTP responses and metrics.
Explains that programmer errors should be logged with high severity and may require process restart because the application state can be unreliable.
Mentions using custom error classes or flags such as isOperational to avoid treating all errors as equal.
Connects classification to alerting, retries, response safety, and whether to continue serving traffic.
redFlags: Treats every error as a 500 and continues normally.
Exposes programmer error internals to clients.
Retries programmer bugs as if they were transient operational failures.
Crashes the process for every user validation error.
Cannot give concrete examples of each category.

scoringHints.description: Gives clear examples, recovery strategy, HTTP response mapping, logging severity, process restart reasoning, and classification mechanism.
Understands the categories but misses one operational implication such as alerting, retry, or process health.
Can define the terms loosely but cannot apply them to Express API behavior.
Answer does not show recoverability or production ownership judgment.

followUps.question: Classify these errors: invalid request body, database timeout, and Cannot read properties of undefined.
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate can apply the distinction.

followUps.question: Why might a programmer error justify restarting the Node process?

en.title: Operational versus programmer errors
en.displayQuestion: What is the difference between operational errors and programmer errors?
en.displayIntent: Assess error classification, recovery decisions, and production handling judgment.
guidance: Define both categories with concrete API examples.
Explain which errors become controlled client responses.
Discuss when a bug may require process restart.
Connect classification to logging, alerting, metrics, and retries.
commonMistakes: Returning 500 for every error.
Leaking bug details to clients.
Retrying programming bugs.
Crashing for normal user input errors.

vi.title: Operational error và programmer error
vi.displayQuestion: Sự khác biệt giữa operational error và programmer error là gì?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá cách phân loại lỗi, quyết định recovery và judgment khi vận hành production.
guidance: Định nghĩa cả hai nhóm bằng ví dụ API cụ thể.
Giải thích lỗi nào chuyển thành response có kiểm soát cho client.
Nói khi nào bug có thể cần restart process.
Liên hệ phân loại lỗi với logging, alerting, metrics và retry.
commonMistakes: Trả 500 cho mọi lỗi.
Leak chi tiết bug cho client.
Retry lỗi do bug code.
Crash process vì lỗi input bình thường của user.

ja.title: operational error と programmer error
ja.displayQuestion: operational error と programmer error の違いは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: error classification, recovery decision, production handling judgment を評価する。
guidance: 両方の category を具体的な API 例で定義する。
どの error が controlled client response になるか説明する。
bug が process restart を必要とする場合を議論する。
classification を logging, alerting, metrics, retry と結びつける。
commonMistakes: すべての error に 500 を返す。
bug の詳細を client に漏らす。
programming bug を retry する。
通常の user input error で process を crash させる。

sourceReferences.note: Used for process-level safety context around uncaught exceptions and shutdown.

intent: Check whether the candidate can handle unmatched routes with a 404 middleware and place it correctly relative to routes and error handlers.
primaryQuestion: How do you handle 404 errors for routes that do not exist in Express?
expectedSignals: Explains that a 404 is handled by a catch-all normal middleware registered after all routes.
Mentions either sending a consistent 404 response directly or creating a NotFoundError and passing it to next(err).
Places the 404 handler before the centralized error handler if it delegates via next(err).
Understands that 404 is not thrown automatically as an exception by Express for unmatched routes.
Keeps API response format consistent with the centralized error contract.
redFlags: Places the 404 handler before real routes, causing valid routes to be shadowed.
Assumes the global error handler automatically receives unmatched routes without a 404 middleware.
Returns 500 for every unknown route.
Uses a broad wildcard route too early and breaks static files or mounted routers.

scoringHints.description: Explains catch-all order, direct 404 versus NotFoundError delegation, centralized formatting, and route shadowing risks.
Knows to add a final 404 middleware but misses delegation or response consistency detail.
Can return 404 in individual routes but lacks a global unmatched-route strategy.
Answer would mishandle unmatched routes or break valid route ordering.

followUps.question: Where exactly would you place the 404 handler relative to app.use('/api', router) and the error handler?
Would you send the 404 response directly or call next(new NotFoundError())? What trade-off are you making?

en.title: Handling unmatched Express routes
en.displayQuestion: How do you handle 404 errors for routes that do not exist in Express?
en.displayIntent: Check catch-all 404 middleware placement and response consistency.
guidance: Register the 404 middleware after all real routes.
Either send the 404 directly or create a NotFoundError.
Place delegated 404 errors before the centralized error handler.
Keep the API error shape consistent.
commonMistakes: Putting the catch-all before routes.
Expecting Express to throw unmatched routes automatically.
Returning 500 for unknown URLs.
Breaking mounted routers with an early wildcard.

vi.title: Xử lý route không tồn tại trong Express
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để xử lý lỗi 404 (route không tồn tại) trong Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách đặt catch-all 404 middleware và giữ response nhất quán.
guidance: Đăng ký 404 middleware sau toàn bộ route thật.
Hoặc gửi 404 trực tiếp, hoặc tạo NotFoundError.
Nếu delegate 404 qua next(err), đặt nó trước centralized error handler.
Giữ error shape của API nhất quán.
commonMistakes: Đặt catch-all trước routes.
Kỳ vọng Express tự throw unmatched route.
Trả 500 cho URL không tồn tại.
Làm hỏng mounted router bằng wildcard quá sớm.

ja.title: Express の unmatched route handling
ja.displayQuestion: Express で存在しない route の 404 error はどう処理しますか。
ja.displayIntent: catch-all 404 middleware の配置と response consistency を確認する。
guidance: 404 middleware はすべての実 route の後に登録する。
404 を直接返すか NotFoundError を作る。
next(err) で委譲する場合は centralized error handler の前に置く。
API error shape を統一する。
commonMistakes: catch-all を routes より前に置く。
Express が unmatched route を自動 throw すると考える。
unknown URL に 500 を返す。
早すぎる wildcard で mounted router を壊す。

sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware ordering and catch-all flow.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling

intent: Verify whether the candidate understands where to register error-handling middleware in an Express application file.
primaryQuestion: Where should error-handling middleware be placed in app.js?
expectedSignals: States that error-handling middleware should be registered after normal middleware and after route handlers.
Mentions that body parsers, request logging, auth setup, routers, and 404 handling usually come before the global error handler.
Explains that Express executes middleware in registration order, so an error handler placed too early cannot catch errors from later routes in the intended way.
Mentions that multiple error handlers can be chained for logging, transformation, and final response, but order still matters.
Understands that route-specific error handlers can exist, but a final global handler should be near the end.
redFlags: Places the global error handler before routers.
Assumes Express searches the whole app for an error handler regardless of registration order.
Registers 404 handling after the final error response handler and expects it to run.
Mixes normal middleware and error middleware without understanding their separate signatures.

scoringHints.description: Explains app.js ordering from parsers/routes/404 to final error handler and understands chained error middleware.
Knows the error handler goes last but misses 404 or multi-error-handler nuance.
Has memorized 'put it at the bottom' without reasoning about registration order.
Answer would place handlers in an order that prevents correct error responses.

followUps.question: What is the typical order of express.json, routers, 404 middleware, and the final error handler?
Why does registration order matter for error middleware?
followUps.purpose: Ensure the candidate understands pipeline execution rather than memorizing placement.

en.title: Error middleware placement
en.displayQuestion: Where should error-handling middleware be placed in app.js?
en.displayIntent: Check Express registration order and final error-handler placement.
guidance: Place the final error handler after normal middleware and routes.
Include 404 handling before the final error handler when it delegates.
Explain that middleware runs in registration order.
Mention optional chained error handlers for logging and final response.
commonMistakes: Putting the error handler before routers.
Forgetting 404 handling order.
Assuming Express ignores registration order.
Using the wrong middleware signature.

vi.title: Vị trí đặt error middleware
vi.displayQuestion: Nên đặt error handling middleware ở đâu trong file app.js?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra thứ tự đăng ký middleware trong Express và vị trí final error handler.
guidance: Đặt final error handler sau normal middleware và routes.
Đưa 404 handling vào trước final error handler nếu 404 delegate qua next(err).
Giải thích middleware chạy theo thứ tự đăng ký.
Nhắc có thể chain nhiều error handler cho logging và final response.
commonMistakes: Đặt error handler trước router.
Quên thứ tự 404 handling.
Tưởng Express bỏ qua registration order.
Dùng sai middleware signature.

ja.title: error middleware の配置
ja.displayQuestion: app.js では error-handling middleware をどこに置くべきですか。
ja.displayIntent: Express の registration order と final error handler の配置を確認する。
guidance: final error handler は normal middleware と routes の後に置く。
404 を next(err) で委譲する場合は final error handler の前に置く。
middleware は登録順に実行されることを説明する。
logging と final response のために複数 error handler を chain できると述べる。
commonMistakes: error handler を router より前に置く。
404 handling の順序を忘れる。
Express が registration order を無視すると考える。
間違った middleware signature を使う。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for placement of error-handling middleware after routes.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Using middleware
sourceReferences.note: Used for middleware registration order.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can log enough information for debugging while returning safe, sanitized error responses to clients.
primaryQuestion: How do you log errors in Express without exposing details to the client?
expectedSignals: Uses a structured logger in the centralized error handler or a dedicated logging error middleware.
Logs server-side fields such as request ID, method, path, status, safe user identifier, error code, stack, cause, and relevant metadata.
Returns a sanitized client response with a stable error code and generic message for unexpected errors.
Avoids logging or returning secrets, authorization headers, cookies, tokens, passwords, full PII, or large raw request bodies.
Uses environment-aware behavior: stack traces may be visible in local development but not in production.
Mentions correlation IDs, tracing, metrics, and alerting for high-severity or high-rate failures.
redFlags: Returns err.message and err.stack directly to production clients for all errors.
Logs complete headers and body without redaction.
Suppresses logging to avoid exposing details, leaving no debugging trail.
Logs the same error many times at every layer and creates noisy alerts.
Does not include request context, making logs hard to correlate.

scoringHints.description: Balances structured server-side diagnostics, safe client responses, redaction, correlation IDs, environment behavior, and alerting.
Separates logs from client responses but misses one production detail such as redaction, correlation, or alerting.
Knows not to expose stacks but logging strategy is incomplete or not actionable.
Answer would leak sensitive data or leave the team unable to debug errors.

followUps.question: What fields would you include in an error log so an incident can be investigated quickly?
Why is logging the full request body dangerous on public API endpoints?

en.title: Logging errors without leaking details
en.displayQuestion: How do you log errors in Express without exposing details to the client?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate production-safe diagnostics, redaction, and client response hygiene.
guidance: Log detailed information server-side with structure and correlation IDs.
Return sanitized responses with stable error codes.
Redact secrets, tokens, cookies, passwords, and sensitive body fields.
Use environment-specific stack exposure and alerting.
commonMistakes: Returning stack traces in production.
Logging raw sensitive request data.
Suppressing logs completely.
Leaving logs without request context.

vi.title: Log lỗi mà không leak chi tiết
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để log lỗi trong Express mà không expose chi tiết cho client?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá cách tạo diagnostic an toàn cho production, redaction và hygiene của response client.
guidance: Log chi tiết phía server bằng structured log và correlation ID.
Trả response đã sanitize với error code ổn định.
Redact secret, token, cookie, password và field nhạy cảm trong body.
Dùng stack exposure và alerting theo môi trường.
commonMistakes: Trả stack trace ở production.
Log raw request data nhạy cảm.
Tắt log hoàn toàn.
Log thiếu request context.

ja.title: 詳細を漏らさず error log を残す
ja.displayQuestion: Express で client に詳細を expose せず error を log するにはどうしますか。
ja.displayIntent: production-safe diagnostics, redaction, client response hygiene を評価する。
guidance: server-side には structured log と correlation ID で詳細を残す。
client には stable error code を含む sanitized response を返す。
secret, token, cookie, password, body の sensitive field を redact する。
environment ごとの stack exposure と alerting を使う。
commonMistakes: production で stack trace を返す。
sensitive request data を raw log する。
log を完全に抑止する。
request context のない log を残す。

sourceReferences.note: Used for production logging and error handling context.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for centralized error middleware behavior and response lifecycle.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands Node.js process-level error events and how they relate to Express request-level error handling.
primaryQuestion: What are process.on('uncaughtException') and process.on('unhandledRejection') used for?
expectedSignals: Explains that uncaughtException fires when an exception reaches the event loop without being caught.
Explains that unhandledRejection fires when a Promise is rejected without a handler within the event loop turn.
States that these are process-level safety nets for logging, metrics, cleanup, and controlled shutdown, not normal Express request error handling.
Mentions that after an uncaught exception the process may be in an unsafe state and should usually stop accepting traffic and restart under a process manager or orchestrator.
Mentions that unhandled rejections should be fixed at the source by catching or forwarding to next(err), not ignored globally.
Discusses graceful shutdown steps such as stop readiness, server.close, cleanup connections, flush logs, and exit with an appropriate code.
redFlags: Uses process.on handlers as the main way to send HTTP error responses.
Continues serving traffic indefinitely after uncaughtException.
Suppresses unhandled rejections globally without fixing the rejected promise.
Performs long async recovery inside uncaughtException and assumes the app is healthy.
Does not alert or restart after fatal process-level failures.

scoringHints.description: Separates process-level safety nets from request error handling, explains both events, and gives a controlled shutdown and restart strategy.
Knows the events are for uncaught errors and rejected promises but misses shutdown or request-level separation detail.
Has used the events for logging but treats them as a normal error recovery mechanism.
Answer would hide fatal failures or leave a corrupted process serving traffic.

followUps.question: What would your service do immediately after receiving an uncaughtException in production?
Why should unhandledRejection not be treated as a substitute for next(err) in Express routes?

en.title: Node process-level error events
en.displayQuestion: What are process.on('uncaughtException') and process.on('unhandledRejection') used for?
en.displayIntent: Assess process-level failure handling and its boundary with Express request error handling.
guidance: Define both Node.js process events.
Emphasize they are safety nets, not normal route error handling.
Discuss logging, alerting, cleanup, graceful shutdown, and restart.
Explain why errors should still be handled at their source.
commonMistakes: Using process events to format HTTP responses.
Continuing after uncaughtException as if healthy.
Ignoring rejected promises globally.
Skipping alerts and restart strategy.

vi.title: Process-level error event trong Node
vi.displayQuestion: process.on('uncaughtException') và process.on('unhandledRejection') dùng để làm gì?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá cách xử lý lỗi cấp process và ranh giới với error handling theo request trong Express.
guidance: Định nghĩa cả hai event của Node.js process.
Nhấn mạnh đây là safety net, không phải cách xử lý lỗi route thông thường.
Nói về logging, alerting, cleanup, graceful shutdown và restart.
Giải thích vì sao lỗi vẫn phải được xử lý tại nguồn phát sinh.
commonMistakes: Dùng process event để format HTTP response.
Tiếp tục chạy sau uncaughtException như app vẫn khỏe.
Ignore rejected promise bằng global handler.
Bỏ qua alert và chiến lược restart.

ja.title: Node の process-level error events
ja.displayQuestion: process.on('uncaughtException') と process.on('unhandledRejection') は何に使いますか。
ja.displayIntent: process-level failure handling と Express request error handling の境界を評価する。
guidance: Node.js process の両 event を定義する。
通常の route error handling ではなく safety net だと強調する。
logging, alerting, cleanup, graceful shutdown, restart を説明する。
error は発生元で処理すべき理由を説明する。
commonMistakes: process event で HTTP response を format する。
uncaughtException 後も healthy として継続する。
global handler で rejected promise を無視する。
alert と restart strategy を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js docs: Process events
sourceReferences.note: Used for uncaughtException and unhandledRejection semantics and warnings.

sourceReferences.label: Express performance best practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for production process reliability and restart context.

intent: Check whether the candidate can design custom Error classes that carry HTTP and application metadata safely through Express error handling.
primaryQuestion: How do you create a custom Error class in Node.js or Express?
expectedSignals: Extends the built-in Error class and calls super(message).
Sets useful fields such as name, statusCode or status, code, isOperational, details, and cause when appropriate.
Uses Object.setPrototypeOf(this, ClassName.prototype) in TypeScript/transpiled environments if needed to preserve instanceof behavior.
Uses Error.captureStackTrace when available to keep stack traces clean.
Separates safe public message and internal details so the error handler can avoid leaking implementation data.
Shows concrete subclasses such as BadRequestError, UnauthorizedError, NotFoundError, or ValidationError.
redFlags: Throws plain strings or arbitrary objects instead of Error instances.
Stores secrets or raw database errors directly in public message fields.
Does not call super(message), losing stack and message behavior.
Creates many custom classes without a consistent base shape used by the error handler.
Relies on HTTP status text alone and loses application-specific error codes.

scoringHints.description: Designs a base AppError with HTTP status, stable code, operational flag, safe public message, optional details/cause, and correct prototype/stack behavior.
Can extend Error and set statusCode but misses prototype, cause, or public/private detail separation.
Has thrown custom errors but lacks a consistent shape for centralized handling.
Answer would make errors hard to classify, log, or map to responses.

followUps.question: What fields would you put on a base AppError class and which of them are safe to return to clients?
Why is throwing a string worse than throwing an Error instance?
followUps.purpose: Check stack, type, and consistency reasoning.

en.title: Custom Error classes for Express
en.displayQuestion: How do you create a custom Error class in Node.js or Express?
en.displayIntent: Check whether errors carry enough metadata for centralized handling without leaking internals.
guidance: Extend Error and call super(message).
Add statusCode, stable code, isOperational, details, and cause deliberately.
Preserve prototype and stack behavior where needed.
Separate public response data from internal diagnostic data.
commonMistakes: Throwing strings.
Leaking internal messages as public messages.
Forgetting super(message).
Creating inconsistent error shapes.

vi.title: Custom Error class cho Express
vi.displayQuestion: Làm thế nào để tạo một custom Error class trong Node.js/Express?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra lỗi có đủ metadata cho centralized handling mà không leak chi tiết nội bộ hay không.
guidance: Extend Error và gọi super(message).
Thêm statusCode, code ổn định, isOperational, details và cause một cách có chủ đích.
Giữ đúng prototype và stack behavior khi cần.
Tách dữ liệu public response khỏi dữ liệu diagnostic nội bộ.
commonMistakes: Throw string.
Leak internal message thành public message.
Quên gọi super(message).
Tạo error shape không nhất quán.

ja.title: Express の custom Error class
ja.displayQuestion: Node.js/Express では custom Error class をどう作りますか。
ja.displayIntent: centralized handling に必要な metadata を持ちつつ内部詳細を漏らさない error 設計を確認する。
guidance: Error を extend し super(message) を呼ぶ。
statusCode, stable code, isOperational, details, cause を意図的に追加する。
必要に応じて prototype と stack behavior を保つ。
public response data と internal diagnostic data を分ける。
commonMistakes: string を throw する。
internal message を public message として漏らす。
super(message) を忘れる。
一貫しない error shape を作る。

sourceReferences.label: Node.js docs: Error cause and stack context
sourceReferences.note: Used for Node.js Error behavior, stack traces, and cause context.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for passing custom Error instances through Express error middleware.

intent: Assess whether the candidate understands Express 5 improvements to promise-based async error handling and what still remains the developer's responsibility.
primaryQuestion: What did Express 5 improve about async error handling compared with Express 4?
expectedSignals: States that route handlers and middleware returning Promises in Express 5 automatically call next(rejectedValue) when they reject or throw.
Contrasts this with Express 4, where async/await handler errors require try/catch, a wrapper, or a package.
Explains that this reduces boilerplate and lost unhandled promise rejections in request handlers.
Mentions that a centralized error handler is still required to format responses consistently.
Notes that errors outside the request lifecycle, such as background jobs, timers, streams, and event emitters, still need explicit handling.
Mentions migration caution: test route matching, middleware behavior, and error response contracts when upgrading.
redFlags: Claims Express 5 catches every asynchronous error anywhere in the process.
Removes all error handlers because Express 5 forwards promises automatically.
Assumes Express 4 already had identical behavior.
Ignores migration testing for route behavior and error contracts.
Forgets that async work started but not returned or awaited can still escape request handling.

scoringHints.description: Clearly explains automatic promise rejection forwarding, Express 4 contrast, remaining boundaries, centralized handler need, and migration verification.
Knows Express 5 forwards rejected promises but misses lifecycle boundaries or migration risks.
Has heard Express 5 improves async errors but cannot state the exact behavior.
Answer would lead to missing handlers or false confidence in async error coverage.

followUps.question: In Express 5, do you still need app.use((err, req, res, next) => ...)? Why?
followUps.purpose: Check separation between forwarding and formatting.

followUps.question: Will Express 5 catch an error thrown in a background job started from a route after the response is sent?

en.title: Express 5 async error handling
en.displayQuestion: What did Express 5 improve about async error handling compared with Express 4?
en.displayIntent: Verify version-specific understanding of promise rejection forwarding and remaining responsibilities.
guidance: State that Express 5 forwards rejected Promises from returned route handlers and middleware.
Contrast with Express 4 wrappers or try/catch.
Explain that centralized response formatting is still needed.
Mention async work outside request lifecycle and migration testing.
commonMistakes: Thinking Express 5 catches all process async errors.
Removing the error handler.
Assuming Express 4 had the same behavior.
Ignoring unreturned or background async work.

vi.title: Async error handling trong Express 5
vi.displayQuestion: Express 5 cải thiện gì về async error handling so với Express 4?
vi.displayIntent: Xác minh hiểu biết theo version về promise rejection forwarding và trách nhiệm còn lại của developer.
guidance: Nói rõ Express 5 forward rejected Promise từ route handler và middleware được return.
So sánh với Express 4 cần wrapper hoặc try/catch.
Giải thích vẫn cần centralized response formatting.
Nhắc async work ngoài request lifecycle và test khi migration.
commonMistakes: Tưởng Express 5 bắt mọi async error trong process.
Xóa error handler.
Tưởng Express 4 có behavior giống vậy.
Bỏ qua async work không return hoặc chạy background.

ja.title: Express 5 の async error handling
ja.displayQuestion: Express 5 は Express 4 と比べて async error handling をどう改善しましたか。
ja.displayIntent: promise rejection forwarding と残る developer responsibility の version-specific understanding を確認する。
guidance: Express 5 は return された route handler/middleware の rejected Promise を forward すると述べる。
Express 4 では wrapper や try/catch が必要だったと比較する。
centralized response formatting はまだ必要だと説明する。
request lifecycle 外の async work と migration testing に触れる。
commonMistakes: Express 5 が process 内の全 async error を catch すると考える。
error handler を削除する。
Express 4 も同じ behavior だったと思う。
return されない async work や background work を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for Express 5 automatic next(value) behavior on rejected Promises.

sourceReferences.label: Express docs: Moving to Express 5
sourceReferences.note: Used for migration context and Express 5 behavior changes.

intent: Check whether the candidate understands why return next(err) is often used to stop fall-through control flow after delegating an error.
primaryQuestion: When should you use return next(err) instead of just next(err)?
expectedSignals: Explains that next(err) delegates the error but does not automatically stop the current JavaScript function from continuing.
Uses return next(err) when there is code after the call or when inside a conditional branch to avoid fall-through.
Connects this to preventing duplicate responses, extra database work, or both success and error paths running.
Mentions that if next(err) is the final statement in the function, return is not strictly required but is often used for clarity.
Understands that return next(err) does not mean Express waits for a return value; it is a control-flow guard in the current handler.
Mentions similar discipline after res.status(...).json(...) or res.redirect(...) when more code follows.
redFlags: Thinks next(err) automatically exits the current function.
Calls next(err) and then continues to send a response.
Believes return next(err) changes the error handler's response value.
Uses return inconsistently and creates hard-to-follow branches.
Does not connect the issue to 'Cannot set headers after they are sent' bugs.

scoringHints.description: Explains JavaScript fall-through, Express delegation, duplicate-response prevention, and when return is optional versus necessary.
Knows return avoids continuing execution but misses the nuance that Express does not use the returned value.
Has seen the pattern but cannot explain the control-flow reason.
Answer would likely produce duplicate responses or confusing handler flow.

followUps.question: Can you show a route where missing return before next(err) causes a second response to be sent?
Does Express use the value returned by return next(err)? What is the return for?
followUps.purpose: Separate JavaScript control flow from Express error forwarding.

en.title: return next(err) and control flow
en.displayQuestion: When should you use return next(err) instead of just next(err)?
en.displayIntent: Check whether the candidate understands fall-through control flow after delegating errors.
guidance: Explain that next(err) does not exit the current function.
Use return when later code should not run.
Tie this to duplicate response and headers-sent bugs.
Clarify that Express does not use the returned value.
commonMistakes: Assuming next(err) stops execution.
Sending a response after next(err).
Thinking return changes Express response behavior.
Missing the same pattern after res.json or res.redirect.

vi.title: return next(err) và control flow
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào nên return next(err) thay vì chỉ next(err)?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra ứng viên có hiểu fall-through control flow sau khi delegate lỗi hay không.
guidance: Giải thích next(err) không tự thoát khỏi function hiện tại.
Dùng return khi phần code phía sau không được chạy tiếp.
Liên hệ với lỗi duplicate response và headers already sent.
Nói rõ Express không dùng giá trị return đó.
commonMistakes: Tưởng next(err) tự dừng execution.
Gửi response sau khi gọi next(err).
Tưởng return thay đổi response behavior của Express.
Bỏ qua pattern tương tự sau res.json hoặc res.redirect.

ja.title: return next(err) と control flow
ja.displayQuestion: next(err) だけでなく return next(err) を使うべきなのはどんな時ですか。
ja.displayIntent: error を委譲した後の fall-through control flow を理解しているか確認する。
guidance: next(err) は現在の function を自動終了しないと説明する。
後続 code を実行してはいけない場合に return を使う。
duplicate response や headers already sent の bug と結びつける。
Express はその returned value を使わないと説明する。
commonMistakes: next(err) が execution を止めると思う。
next(err) 後に response を送る。
return が Express response behavior を変えると思う。
res.json や res.redirect 後の同じ pattern を見落とす。
